L. 108–136, title XXXV, § 3532(a)(2)117 Stat. 1817Oct. 8, 1996Nov. 24, 200356101(d)46 App.:808(d)(1).56101(e)46 App.:808(d)(2)–(4).In subsection (a), the text of 46 App. U.S.C. 808a is omitted as unnecessary. In paragraph (1), the words “owned by a citizen of the United States” are omitted as unnecessary because ownership by a citizen is a requirement for documentation. See section 12103 as revised by the bill.In subsection (c), before paragraph (1), the words “Notwithstanding any other provision of this subtitle, the Merchant Marine Act, 1936, or any contract with the Secretary made under this subtitle or that Act” are substituted for “Notwithstanding subsection (c)(2) of this section, the Merchant Marine Act, 1936 [46 App. U.S.C. 1101 et seq.], or any contract entered into with the Secretary of Transportation under that Act” because the Merchant Marine Act, 1936, is restated principally in this subtitle, but other provisions of that Act are being neither restated nor repealed.

References in TextThe Merchant Marine Act, 1936, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1) and (c), is , , which was classified principally to chapter 27 (§ 1101 et seq.) of the former Appendix to this title. The Act, with the exception of title V, most of title VI, and sections 301, 801, 802, 809(a), and 909 thereof, was repealed and restated, mainly in this subtitle, by , §§ 8, 19, , , 1710. Title V and sections 301 and 909 of the Act are set out as notes under . Those portions of title VI not repealed by and sections 802 and 809(a) of the Act were repealed by , , . Section 801 of the Act was transferred to by , , . For complete classification of the Act to the Code, see Tables. C, title XXXV, § 3514(b)(2)137 Stat. 810Jan. 1, 2021Dec. 22, 2023Dec. 22, 2023

children.label: i
children.text: a documented vessel owned by a citizen of the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a vessel last documented under the laws of the United States; or

children.label: B
children.text: place under foreign registry, or operate under the authority of a foreign country, a documented vessel or a vessel last documented under the laws of the United States.

children.heading: .—Exceptions
children.label: 2 .—Exceptions
children.text: Paragraph (1)(A) does not apply to a vessel that has been operated only for pleasure or only as a fishing vessel, fish processing vessel, or fish tender vessel (as defined in ).section 2101 of this title

children.heading: .—Approval Before Documentation
children.label: b .—Approval Before Documentation
children.text: To promote financing with respect to a vessel to be documented under chapter 121 of this title, the Secretary may grant approval under subsection (a) before the vessel is documented.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary, in conjunction with the Secretary of Defense, determines that at least one replacement vessel of equal or greater military capability and of a capacity that is equivalent or greater, as measured by deadweight tons, gross tons, or container equivalent units, as appropriate, is documented under chapter 121 of this title by the owner of the vessel placed under foreign registry; and

children.label: B
children.text: the replacement vessel is not more than 10 years old on the date of that documentation; or
an operating agreement covering the vessel under chapter 531 of this title has expired.

children.heading: .—Status of Prohibited Transaction
children.label: d .—Status of Prohibited Transaction
children.text: A charter, sale, or transfer of a vessel, or of an interest in or control of a vessel, in violation of this section is void.

children.heading: .—Criminal penalty
children.label: 1 .—Criminal penalty
children.text: A person that knowingly sells, charters, or transfers a vessel, or an interest in or control of a vessel, in violation of this section shall be fined under title 18, imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both.

children.heading: .—Civil penalty
children.label: 2 .—Civil penalty
children.text: A person that sells, charters, or transfers a vessel, or an interest in or control of a vessel, in violation of this section is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of not more than $10,000 for each violation.

children.label: A
children.text: knowingly sells, charters, or transfers the vessel or an interest in or control of the vessel; or

children.label: B
children.text: places the vessel under foreign registry or operates the vessel under the authority of a foreign country.

children.heading: .—Foreign Vessel Charters for Passenger Vessels
children.label: f .—Foreign Vessel Charters for Passenger Vessels

children.text: a detailed summary of each request for a determination, approval, or confirmation that a vessel charter for a passenger vessel is encompassed by the general approval of time charters issued pursuant to this section or regulations prescribed pursuant to such section; and
the final action of the Administration with respect to such request, after the provision of notice and opportunity for public comment.
